item.title: advice
item.description: Some advice, I’ll update this occasionally. Note that I think most advice applies to everyone. Some people say all advice is bad, to them I say this isn’t advice despite being titled advice, and is instead just reminders of obvious facts about the world.

item.title: Calvin and Hobbes
item.description: Calvin and Hobbes is truly a top tier piece of media for me. Maybe mostly due to fortune, where a family friend gifted a box set of two decades of Calvin and Hobbes to me after occupying my room for some time.

item.title: projects
item.description: Whole lotta projects that I’ve started or ideated. I don’t care for getting scooped so if you’re interested, please go ahead and do them! Then tell me about it so I can thank you / ask you about how it went.

item.title: Deciding if something is on the frontier
item.description: How do you decide if something is on the frontier? I’m a little bit confused. I’ll often want to do analysis which says: take a set of data points, figure out which of the ones are, on the frontier or that are state-of-the-art at the time they are made available, and then plot a trend line so that you can accurately guess what the frontier will be in the future.

item.title: stuff
item.description: List of stuff I carry around or otherwise use that others might find useful or that I might want to reflect on later in life. Stuff on my person iPhone 12 Wallet: my ~4 most important cards (including my ID) + keys on an attached keychain.

item.title: fog-of-war chess
item.description: Fog-of-war chess is by far, my favorite game in the world. Fog-of-war is a variant of chess (available on chess.com) where you are only able to see the squares your pieces are or where your pieces can move.

item.title: disclaimers
item.description: This is a place for disclaimers, disclosures, credits, and hedges of all sorts. Fairly often I will fall back into the bad habit of being disincentivized from writing or generally broadcasting my thoughts publicly by various concerns that can be mitigated by some kind of kneecapping, disclaimers, or hedges.

item.title: Post-AGI equilibria
item.description: First, I’ll maybe add the set of questions that I’m hoping to answer during the conference. Preferably fairly concrete, to increase the likelihood that I successfully answer them.

item.title: do
item.description: you can just do things, you should do (good) things (duh), but should is bad, so ideally, you want to do things. a potential reader remarks: “but I don’t want to do the things that I should do” Whatever! There are things you do want to do.

item.title: models of rsi
item.description: Status: rough draft What even is RSI? Here’s my attempt at defining it. TLDR I think it means humans out of the loop of AI development Below are some models of how RSI comes about.
